description: Bid 2C over 1NT as Garbage Stayman — a weak-hand escape with 4-4 majors and short clubs
purpose: Use Stayman as an escape mechanism with weak hands containing both majors and at most one club, planning to pass any response

authority.label: Bridge Guys — Garbage Stayman as a 4-4 majors, short-club escape variant

teaching.tradeoff: Garbage Stayman sacrifices the standard HCP requirement for Stayman to allow weak hands with short clubs to escape 1NT.
teaching.principle: With a weak hand, both majors, and short clubs, bid 2C planning to pass any response — you will land in a better contract than 1NT.
commonMistakes: Don't use Garbage Stayman without at least 4-4 in both majors and short clubs — you need to be happy passing any 2-level response
Remember that partner may jump to game if maximum — be prepared for this possibility even with a weak hand

Stayman is the preferred convention

clauses.rationale: Garbage Stayman is for weak hands only
must be safe if opener bids 2♥
must be safe if opener bids 2♠
short clubs ensures 4+ diamonds as escape after 2♦ denial

definitions.description: Eligible for Stayman (4+ card major AND 8+ HCP)
Stayman preferred (eligible AND either no 5-card major OR 5-4 in both majors)
Posterior probability that N/S have an 8+ card major fit
Posterior probability that opener has balanced shape
Posterior probability that opener has a second 4-card major
